skill.name: wiki-vitepress
skill.description: Packages generated wiki Markdown into a VitePress static site with dark theme, dark-mode Mermaid diagrams with click-to-zoom, and production build output.
skill.summary: Transform wiki Markdown files into a polished VitePress static site with dark theme and interactive Mermaid diagrams.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer detected 69 patterns but 68 are false positives from Markdown documentation (code fences mistaken for shell execution, crypto references in comments). One legitimate concern: innerHTML assignment at line 112 uses self-generated content (low risk). Safe to publish with documentation warning.

low_findings.title: innerHTML Usage
low_findings.description: innerHTML assignment at line 112 sets modal content from el.outerHTML. While content is self-generated (not user input), innerHTML is generally discouraged due to potential XSS risks if content source changes.

content.user_title: Build VitePress Sites from Wiki Markdown
content.value_statement: Transform generated wiki Markdown files into a polished, browsable VitePress static site. Includes dark theme, dark-mode Mermaid diagrams with click-to-zoom, and production-ready build output.
actual_capabilities: Generate VitePress project structure with config, theme, and pages
Configure dark-mode Mermaid diagrams with custom theme variables
Apply three-layer CSS fix for dark-mode Mermaid rendering
Add click-to-zoom functionality for Mermaid diagrams
Fix Markdown syntax issues for Vue template compatibility
limitations: Requires Node.js and npm to be installed for building the site
Mermaid diagrams require polling due to async rendering behavior
Does not support isCustomElement compiler option for generic parameters
DOM manipulation uses setup() not enhanceApp() due to SSR constraints

use_cases.title: Technical Documentation Site
use_cases.description: Build a professional documentation site from wiki Markdown files with dark theme and interactive diagrams.

use_cases.title: Knowledge Base Export
use_cases.description: Convert internal wiki content into a browsable static site for team knowledge sharing.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering teams documenting architecture and processes

use_cases.title: Open Source Project Docs
use_cases.description: Generate polished documentation sites with Mermaid diagrams for GitHub repositories.
use_cases.target_user: Open source maintainainers publishing project documentation

prompt_templates.title: Basic VitePress Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a VitePress site structure in wiki-site/ with dark theme config and copy all generated .md wiki files into the project.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Mermaid Dark Mode
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up the VitePress config with Mermaid plugin and configure dark theme variables for mermaid.themeVariables including primaryColor, nodeBorder, and background colors.

prompt_templates.title: Add Click-to-Zoom for Diagrams
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement click-to-zoom functionality for Mermaid diagrams by wrapping .mermaid containers with event listeners that open fullscreen modals on click.

prompt_templates.title: Build Production Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Run post-processing to fix Markdown syntax issues, then build the VitePress site with npm run docs:build and output the dist folder.

output_examples.input: Generate VitePress site from wiki pages
output_examples.output: Created wiki-site/ directory with .vitepress/config.mts (dark theme, Mermaid configured), custom.css (Mermaid dark styles), theme/index.ts (click-to-zoom setup), and 12 wiki pages. Site builds successfully to .vitepress/dist/.

output_examples.input: Configure Mermaid diagrams for dark mode
output_examples.output: Applied three-layer dark mode fix: 1) themeVariables in config.mts with custom colors, 2) CSS overrides targeting .mermaid SVG elements, 3) JavaScript polling in theme/index.ts to replace inline styles. All diagrams render correctly in dark mode.

best_practices: Use setup() with onMounted for DOM manipulation, not enhanceApp() which runs during SSR
Apply polling for Mermaid SVG elements since they render asynchronously
Ensure all wiki pages have YAML frontmatter with title and description
anti_patterns: Do not use isCustomElement compiler option for bare generic parameters - causes crashes
Avoid relying solely on CSS for Mermaid dark mode - inline styles require JavaScript replacement
Do not attempt DOM access during SSR phase - document is undefined

faq.question: Why do Mermaid diagrams not render in dark mode?
faq.answer: Mermaid applies inline styles with high specificity. You need the three-layer fix: themeVariables in config, CSS overrides with !important, and JavaScript to replace inline styles on rendered SVGs.

faq.question: Why use setup() instead of enhanceApp() for DOM manipulation?
faq.answer: enhanceApp() runs during server-side rendering where document does not exist. setup() with onMounted only executes in the browser where DOM is available.

faq.question: How do I enable click-to-zoom for diagrams?
faq.answer: Add event listeners to .mermaid elements that create a fullscreen modal div containing the SVG when clicked. The modal should close when clicked and use scale transform for zoom effect.

faq.question: What Markdown syntax issues need fixing before build?
faq.answer: Replace br/ with br for Vue compatibility, wrap bare T generic parameters in backticks outside code fences, and ensure every page has YAML frontmatter with title and description.

faq.question: Where does the built site output go?
faq.answer: The production build outputs to wiki-site/.vitepress/dist/ directory after running npm run docs:build.

faq.question: Can I use this with light mode instead of dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes, change appearance to 'dark-mode' in config.mts and adjust the Mermaid theme variables and CSS colors for light backgrounds and dark text.
